revision.text:
'''金刀比羅宮'''（ことひらぐう）は、[[東京都]][[港区]]虎ノ門にある[[神社]]。敷地内には、金刀比羅宮との複合施設として高層オフィスビルの虎ノ門琴平タワーがあり、社務所や神楽殿といった施設と一体化している。
==歴史== *[[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）、[[讃岐]][[丸亀藩]]の藩主[[京極高和]]が芝・三田の[[江戸藩邸]]に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を勧請。 *[[延宝]]7年（[[1679年]]）、丸亀藩江戸藩邸の移転とともに現在の虎ノ門に遷座。 *[[平成13年]]（[[2001年]]）11月28日、東京都選定歴史的建造物に選定される。 *[[平成16年]]（[[2004年]]）11月30日、虎ノ門琴平タワーが竣工。
==祭神== *[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]
==所在地== *東京都港区虎ノ門1-2-7
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ 虎ノ門・金刀比羅宮] *[http://www.kotohiratower.com/ 虎ノ門琴平タワー（三井不動産）]
[[Category:東京都の神社|ことひらぐう]] [[Category:金毘羅神社|ことひらしんしや]]

'''金刀比羅宮'''（ことひらぐう）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]]虎ノ門にある[[神社]]。敷地内には、金刀比羅宮との複合施設として高層オフィスビルの虎ノ門琴平タワーがあり、社務所や神楽殿といった施設と一体化している。
== 歴史 == *[[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）、[[讃岐国|讃岐]][[丸亀藩]]の藩主[[京極高和]]が芝・三田の[[江戸藩邸]]に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を勧請。 *[[延宝]]7年（[[1679年]]）、丸亀藩江戸藩邸の移転とともに現在の虎ノ門に遷座。 *[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）11月28日、東京都選定歴史的建造物に選定される。 *平成16年（[[2004年]]）11月30日、虎ノ門琴平タワーが竣工。
== 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]
== 所在地 == *東京都港区虎ノ門1-2-7
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ 虎ノ門・金刀比羅宮] *[http://www.kotohiratower.com/ 虎ノ門琴平タワー（三井不動産）]
[[Category:東京都の神社|ことひらぐう]] [[Category:金毘羅神社|ことひらしんしや]]

'''金刀比羅宮'''（ことひらぐう）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]]虎ノ門にある[[神社]]。敷地内には、金刀比羅宮との複合施設として高層オフィスビルの虎ノ門琴平タワーがあり、社務所や神楽殿といった施設と一体化している。
== 歴史 == *[[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）、[[讃岐国|讃岐]][[丸亀藩]]の藩主[[京極高和]]が芝・三田の[[江戸藩邸]]に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を勧請。 *[[延宝]]7年（[[1679年]]）、丸亀藩江戸藩邸の移転とともに現在の虎ノ門に遷座。 *[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）11月28日、東京都選定歴史的建造物に選定される。 *平成16年（[[2004年]]）11月30日、虎ノ門琴平タワーが竣工。
== 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]
== 所在地 == *東京都港区虎ノ門1-2-7
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ 虎ノ門・金刀比羅宮] *[http://www.kotohiratower.com/ 虎ノ門琴平タワー（三井不動産）]
[[Category:東京都の神社|ことひらくう]] [[Category:金毘羅神社|ことひらしんしや]]

'''金刀比羅宮'''（ことひらぐう）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]]虎ノ門にある[[神社]]。敷地内には、金刀比羅宮との複合施設として高層オフィスビルの虎ノ門琴平タワーがあり、社務所や神楽殿といった施設と一体化している。 昨今の[[嫌煙]]傾向により喫煙場所を追いやられた愛煙家が、昼夜問わず境内の灰皿周辺で喫煙しているため、境内全体が非常に煙く、[[受動喫煙]]を嫌う嫌煙家や、近隣にある[[虎の門病院]]患者などが近寄りがたい状況となっている。
== 歴史 == *[[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）、[[讃岐国|讃岐]][[丸亀藩]]の藩主[[京極高和]]が芝・三田の[[江戸藩邸]]に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を勧請。 *[[延宝]]7年（[[1679年]]）、丸亀藩江戸藩邸の移転とともに現在の虎ノ門に遷座。 *[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）11月28日、[[東京都選定歴史的建造物]]に選定される。 *平成16年（[[2004年]]）11月30日、虎ノ門琴平タワーが竣工。
== 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]
== 所在地 == *東京都港区虎ノ門1-2-7
== 交通 == [[東京地下鉄銀座線]][[虎ノ門駅]]より徒歩1分
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ 虎ノ門・金刀比羅宮] *[http://www.kotohiratower.com/ 虎ノ門琴平タワー（三井不動産）]

'''金刀比羅宮'''（ことひらぐう）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]]虎ノ門にある[[神社]]。敷地内には、金刀比羅宮との複合施設として高層オフィスビルの虎ノ門琴平タワーがあり、社務所や神楽殿といった施設と一体化している。 境内にはこの近辺で数少ない喫煙所があるため、日夜問わず境内に煙が漂っており、神社にも関わらず、近隣の[[虎の門病院]]患者や、嫌煙者が近寄りがたい状況となっている。

== 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]
== 所在地 == *東京都港区虎ノ門1-2-7
== 交通 == [[東京地下鉄銀座線]][[虎ノ門駅]]より徒歩1分

'''金刀比羅宮'''（ことひらぐう）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]]虎ノ門一丁目にある[[神社]]。敷地内には、金刀比羅宮との複合施設として高層オフィスビルの虎ノ門琴平タワーがあり、社務所や神楽殿といった施設と一体化している。
== 歴史 == *[[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）、[[讃岐国|讃岐]][[丸亀藩]]の藩主[[京極高和]]が芝・三田の[[江戸藩邸]]に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を勧請。 *[[延宝]]7年（[[1679年]]）、丸亀藩江戸藩邸の移転とともに現在の虎ノ門に遷座。 *[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）11月28日、[[東京都選定歴史的建造物]]に選定される。 *平成16年（[[2004年]]）11月30日、虎ノ門琴平タワーが竣工。
== 祭神 == *[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]
== 所在地 == *東京都港区虎ノ門一丁目2番7号
== 交通 == [[東京地下鉄銀座線]][[虎ノ門駅]]より徒歩1分
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ 虎ノ門・金刀比羅宮] *[http://www.kotohiratower.com/ 虎ノ門琴平タワー（三井不動産）]

朔日祭 *10日 - 十日祭（[[月次祭]]） | *09日 - 九日祭 *20日 - 二十日祭 }} ;その他 *[[1月|01月]]01日 - [[歳旦祭]] *01月09日 - 初こんぴら前日祭 *01月10日 - 初こんぴら祭：境内では[[江戸の里神楽|里神楽]]の奉納、参道では七福神の行列や[[屋台|露店]]が並ぶ *[[2月|02月]]03日 - 節分追儺祭／[[節分#豆まき|福豆撒き]]：神楽殿にて神職が福豆撒き *02月11日 - [[天長祭]] *12月30日 - 年越し大祓式／鎮火祭／除夜祭 == 文化財 == ;都選定歴史的建造物 - 平成13年選定 ;ぼたん 建設年 - 昭和26年（1951年）、設計者 - 伊東忠太、構造規模 - [[木造]]1階、概要 - 讃岐丸亀藩主の讃岐の金刀比羅権現を江戸藩邸に屋敷社として勧請した建物である。第二次世界大戦で焼けたため、権現造りの拝殿・幣殿は昭和26年に再建された。[http://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm 東京都都市整備局] - 都選定歴史的建造物一覧（平成25年8月末日現在）。 == ギャラリー == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|公道より一之鳥居を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|一之鳥居を潜り拝殿を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|手水舎（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|拝殿（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|南東側から拝殿を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|神楽殿（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|本殿の北側通路（2010年5月14日撮影） == 交通 == [[東京メトロ銀座線]][[虎ノ門駅]]より徒歩1分 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ 虎ノ門・金刀比羅宮] *[http://www.kotohiratower.com/ 虎ノ門琴平タワー（三井不動産）] {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらくう}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[Category:東京都港区の歴史]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社|ことひらくう とうきようとみなとく]] [[Category:虎ノ門]] [[Category:丸亀藩]]

（2010年5月14日撮影） |所在地 = [[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[虎ノ門]]一丁目2番地7号 |緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 40 | 緯度秒 = 10.6 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 44 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = [[大物主神]] [[崇徳天皇]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧府社 |創建 = 1660年 |本殿 = |別名 = 虎ノ門金刀比羅宮 |札所等 = |例祭　 = [[]] |神事 = }} '''金刀比羅宮'''（ことひらぐう）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[虎ノ門]]一丁目にある[[神社]]。敷地内には、金刀比羅宮との複合施設として高層オフィスビルの虎ノ門琴平タワーがあり、社務所や神楽殿といった施設と一体化している。 == 歴史 == * [[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）、[[讃岐国|讃岐]][[丸亀藩]]の藩主[[京極高和]]が芝・三田の[[江戸藩邸]]に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を勧請。 * [[延宝]]7年（[[1679年]]）、丸亀藩江戸藩邸の移転とともに現在の虎ノ門に遷座。 * [[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）11月28日、[[東京都選定歴史的建造物]]に選定される。 * 平成16年（[[2004年]]）11月30日、虎ノ門琴平タワーが竣工。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主神]] * [[崇徳天皇]] == 年間祭典 == ;毎月斎行する祭典 {{表2列| * 01日 - [[天長祭]] * 12月30日 - 年越し大祓式／鎮火祭／除夜祭 == 文化財 == ;都選定歴史的建造物 - 平成13年選定 ;ぼたん 建設年 - 昭和26年（1951年）、設計者 - 伊東忠太、構造規模 - [[木造]]1階、概要 - 讃岐丸亀藩主の讃岐の金刀比羅権現を江戸藩邸に屋敷社として勧請した建物である。第二次世界大戦で焼けたため、権現造りの拝殿・幣殿は昭和26年に再建された。[http://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm 東京都都市整備局] - 都選定歴史的建造物一覧（平成25年8月末日現在）。 == ギャラリー == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|公道より一之鳥居を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|一之鳥居を潜り拝殿を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|手水舎（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|拝殿（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|南東側から拝殿を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|神楽殿（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|本殿の北側通路（2010年5月14日撮影） == 交通 == * [[東京メトロ銀座線]][[虎ノ門駅]]より徒歩1分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ 虎ノ門・金刀比羅宮] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ 虎ノ門琴平タワー（三井不動産）] {{デフォルトソート:ことひらくう}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[Category:東京都港区の歴史]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社|ことひらくう とうきようとみなとく]] [[Category:虎ノ門]] [[Category:丸亀藩]] [[Category:東京都港区の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅宮 |画像 =[[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|280px]]鳥居と拝殿
（2010年5月14日撮影） |所在地 = [[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[虎ノ門]]一丁目2番地7号 |緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 40 | 緯度秒 = 10.6 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 44 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |地図 = Tokyo city |祭神 = [[大物主神]] [[崇徳天皇]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧府社 |創建 = 1660年 |本殿 = |別名 = 虎ノ門金刀比羅宮 |札所等 = |例祭　 = |神事 = }} '''金刀比羅宮'''（ことひらぐう）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[虎ノ門]]一丁目にある[[神社]]。敷地内には、金刀比羅宮との複合施設として高層オフィスビルの[[虎ノ門琴平タワー]]があり、社務所や神楽殿といった施設と一体化している。 == 歴史 == * [[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）、[[讃岐国|讃岐]][[丸亀藩]]の藩主[[京極高和]]が芝・三田の[[江戸藩邸]]に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を勧請。 * [[延宝]]7年（[[1679年]]）、丸亀藩江戸藩邸の移転とともに現在の虎ノ門に遷座。 * [[昭和]]40年（[[1965年]]）、虎門琴平会館ビル建設。 * [[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）11月28日、[[東京都選定歴史的建造物]]に選定される。 * 平成16年（[[2004年]]）11月30日、虎門琴平会館ビルを建て替え、虎ノ門琴平タワーが竣工。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主神]] * [[崇徳天皇]] == 年間祭典 == ;毎月斎行する祭典 {{表2列| * 1日 - 朔日祭 * 10日 - 十日祭（[[月次祭]]） | * 9日 - 九日祭 * 20日 - 二十日祭 }} ;その他 * 1月1日 - [[歳旦祭]] * 1月9日 - 初こんぴら前日祭 * 1月10日 - 初こんぴら祭：境内では[[江戸の里神楽|里神楽]]の奉納、参道では七福神の行列や[[屋台|露店]]が並ぶ * 2月3日 - 節分追儺祭／[[節分#豆まき|福豆撒き]]：神楽殿にて神職が福豆撒き * 2月11日 - [[紀元祭]] * 2月20日 - [[祈年祭]]（としごいのまつり）／喜代住稲荷神社（末社）大祭 * 6月30日 - 夏越し[[大祓|大祓式]]：穢れを人形に移し祓い清める／[[道饗祭]] * 10月9日 - 大祭前日祭 * 10月10日 - 大祭：舞姫が[[浦安の舞]]を奉納、[[おかめ]][[ひょっとこ]]の行列や露店が並ぶ * 11月23日 - [[新嘗祭]] * 12月23日 - [[天長祭]] * 12月30日 - 年越し大祓式／鎮火祭／除夜祭 == 文化財 == ;都選定歴史的建造物 - 平成13年選定 ;ぼたん 建設年 - 昭和26年（1951年）、設計者 - 伊東忠太、構造規模 - [[木造]]1階、概要 - 讃岐丸亀藩主の讃岐の金刀比羅権現を江戸藩邸に屋敷社として勧請した建物である。第二次世界大戦で焼けたため、権現造りの拝殿・幣殿は昭和26年に再建された[http://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm 東京都都市整備局] - 都選定歴史的建造物一覧（平成25年8月末日現在）。 == ギャラリー == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|公道より一之鳥居を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|一之鳥居を潜り拝殿を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|手水舎（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|拝殿（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|南東側から拝殿を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|神楽殿（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|本殿の北側通路（2010年5月14日撮影） == 交通 == * [[東京メトロ銀座線]][[虎ノ門駅]]より徒歩1分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ 虎ノ門・金刀比羅宮] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ 虎ノ門琴平タワー（三井不動産）] {{デフォルトソート:ことひらくう}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[Category:東京都港区の歴史]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社|ことひらくう とうきようとみなとく]] [[Category:虎ノ門]] [[Category:丸亀藩]] [[Category:東京都港区の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅宮 |画像 =[[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|280px]]鳥居と拝殿

朔日祭 * 10日 - 十日祭（[[月次祭]]） | * 9日 - 九日祭 * 20日 - 二十日祭 }} ;その他 * 1月1日 - [[歳旦祭]] * 1月9日 - 初こんぴら前日祭 * 1月10日 - 初こんぴら祭：境内では[[江戸の里神楽|里神楽]]の奉納、参道では七福神の行列や[[屋台|露店]]が並ぶ * 2月3日 - 節分追儺祭／[[節分#豆まき|福豆撒き]]：神楽殿にて神職が福豆撒き * 2月11日 - [[紀元祭]] * 2月20日 - [[祈年祭]]（としごいのまつり）／喜代住稲荷神社（末社）大祭 * 6月30日 - 夏越し[[大祓|大祓式]]：穢れを人形に移し祓い清める／[[道饗祭]] * 10月9日 - 大祭前日祭 * 10月10日 - 大祭：舞姫が[[浦安の舞]]を奉納、[[おかめ]][[ひょっとこ]]の行列や露店が並ぶ * 11月23日 - [[新嘗祭]] * 12月23日 - [[天長祭]] * 12月30日 - 年越し大祓式／鎮火祭／除夜祭 == 文化財 == ;都選定歴史的建造物 - 平成13年選定 ;ぼたん 建設年 - 昭和26年（1951年）、設計者 - [[伊東忠太]]、構造規模 - [[木造]]1階、概要 - 讃岐丸亀藩主の讃岐の金刀比羅権現を江戸藩邸に屋敷社として勧請した建物である。第二次世界大戦で焼けたため、権現造りの拝殿・幣殿は昭和26年に再建された[http://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm 東京都都市整備局] - 都選定歴史的建造物一覧（平成25年8月末日現在）。 == ギャラリー == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|公道より一之鳥居を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|一之鳥居を潜り拝殿を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|手水舎（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|拝殿（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|南東側から拝殿を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|神楽殿（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|本殿の北側通路（2010年5月14日撮影） == 交通 == * [[東京メトロ銀座線]][[虎ノ門駅]]より徒歩1分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ 虎ノ門・金刀比羅宮] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ 虎ノ門琴平タワー（三井不動産）] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{デフォルトソート:ことひらくう}} [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社|ことひらくう とうきようとみなとく]] [[Category:虎ノ門]] [[Category:丸亀藩]] [[Category:東京都選定歴史的建造物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅宮 |画像 =[[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]鳥居と拝殿
（2010年5月14日撮影） {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[虎ノ門]]一丁目2番地7号 |緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 40 | 緯度秒 = 10.6 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 44 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |地図 = Tokyo city |祭神 = [[大物主神]] [[崇徳天皇]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧府社 |創建 = [[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 虎ノ門金刀比羅宮 |札所等 = |例祭　 = |神事 = }} '''金刀比羅宮'''（ことひらぐう）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[虎ノ門]]一丁目にある[[神社]]。敷地内には、金刀比羅宮との複合施設として高層オフィスビルの[[虎ノ門琴平タワー]]があり、社務所や神楽殿といった施設と一体化している。 地名を冠して'''虎ノ門金刀比羅宮'''（とらのもんことひらぐう）とも呼ばれる。 == 歴史 == * [[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）、[[讃岐国|讃岐]][[丸亀藩]]の藩主・[[京極高和]]が芝・三田の[[江戸藩邸]]に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を勧請。 * [[延宝]]7年（[[1679年]]）、丸亀藩江戸藩邸の移転とともに現在の虎ノ門に遷座。 * [[昭和]]40年（[[1965年]]）、虎門琴平会館ビル建設。 * [[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）[[11月28日]]、[[東京都選定歴史的建造物]]に選定される。 * 平成16年（[[2004年]]）[[11月30日]]、虎門琴平会館ビルを建て替え、虎ノ門琴平タワーが竣工。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主神]] * [[崇徳天皇]] == 年間祭典 == ; {{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅宮 |画像 =[[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿（2010年5月14日撮影）
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[虎ノ門]]一丁目2番地7号 |緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 40 | 緯度秒 = 10.6 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 44 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |地図 = Tokyo city |祭神 = [[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[府社]] |創建 = [[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 虎ノ門金刀比羅宮 |札所等 = |例祭　 = |神事 = }} '''金刀比羅宮'''（ことひらぐう）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[虎ノ門]]一丁目にある[[神社]]。敷地内には、金刀比羅宮との複合施設として高層オフィスビルの[[虎ノ門琴平タワー]]があり、社務所や神楽殿といった施設と一体化している。 地名を冠して'''虎ノ門金刀比羅宮'''（とらのもんことひらぐう）とも呼ばれる。 == 歴史 == * [[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）、[[讃岐国|讃岐]][[丸亀藩]]の藩主・[[京極高和]]が芝・三田の[[江戸藩邸]]に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を勧請。 * [[延宝]]7年（[[1679年]]）、丸亀藩江戸藩邸の移転とともに現在の虎ノ門に遷座。 * [[昭和]]40年（[[1965年]]）、虎門琴平会館ビル建設。 * [[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）[[11月28日]]、[[東京都選定歴史的建造物]]に選定される。 * 平成16年（[[2004年]]）[[11月30日]]、虎門琴平会館ビルを建て替え、虎ノ門琴平タワーが竣工。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主神]] * [[崇徳天皇]] == 年間祭典 == ; 毎月斎行する祭典 {{表2列| * 1日 - 朔日祭 * 10日 - 十日祭（[[月次祭]]） | * 9日 - 九日祭 * 20日 - 二十日祭 }} ; その他 * 1月1日 - [[歳旦祭]] * 1月9日 - 初こんぴら前日祭 * 1月10日 - 初こんぴら祭：境内では[[江戸の里神楽|里神楽]]の奉納、参道では七福神の行列や[[屋台|露店]]が並ぶ * 2月3日 - 節分追儺祭／[[節分#豆まき|福豆撒き]]：神楽殿にて神職が福豆撒き * 2月11日 - [[紀元祭]] * 2月20日 - [[祈年祭]]（としごいのまつり）／喜代住稲荷神社（末社）大祭 * 6月30日 - 夏越し[[大祓|大祓式]]：穢れを人形に移し祓い清める／[[道饗祭]] * 10月9日 - 大祭前日祭 * 10月10日 - 大祭：舞姫が[[浦安の舞]]を奉納、[[おかめ]][[ひょっとこ]]の行列や露店が並ぶ * 11月23日 - [[新嘗祭]] * 12月23日 - [[天長祭]] * 12月30日 - 年越し大祓式／鎮火祭／除夜祭 == 文化財 == ; 都選定歴史的建造物 : [[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）選定 ; ぼたん : 建設 - [[昭和]]26年（[[1951年]]）、設計者 - [[伊東忠太]]、構造規模 - [[木構造 (建築)|木造]]1階 : 讃岐丸亀藩主の讃岐の金刀比羅権現を江戸藩邸に屋敷社として勧請した建物である。[[第二次世界大戦]]で焼けたため、権現造りの拝殿・幣殿は昭和26年（1951年）に再建された[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm 東京都都市整備局] - 都選定歴史的建造物一覧（平成25年8月末日現在）。 == ギャラリー == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|公道より一之鳥居を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|一之鳥居を潜り拝殿を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|手水舎（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|拝殿（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|南東側から拝殿を見る（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|神楽殿（2010年5月14日撮影） File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|本殿の北側通路（2010年5月14日撮影） == 交通 == * [[東京メトロ銀座線]][[虎ノ門駅]]より徒歩1分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ 虎ノ門・金刀比羅宮] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ 虎ノ門琴平タワー（三井不動産）] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{デフォルトソート:ことひらくう}} [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社|ことひらくう とうきようとみなとく]] [[Category:虎ノ門]] [[Category:丸亀藩]] [[Category:東京都選定歴史的建造物]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | Image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == Annual Festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} [[Category:Shrines in Minato]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Toranomon]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]]

{{Shrine | Name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | Image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shrines in Minato]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Toranomon]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]]

Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shrines in Minato]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Toranomon]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]]

revision.comment: adding new pages tag because that is one I use for finding new links
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | Image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == Annual Festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == Annual Festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == Annual Festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == Annual Festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == Annual Festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == Annual Festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Shrine | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Shrine | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii {{Shrine | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Shrine | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] *
