{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February qq - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February qq - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|ja|虎ノ門}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|ja|万治}} 3 ({{ill|1660|ja|1660年}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|ja|丸亀藩}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 7 ({{ill|1679|ja|1679年}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 40 ({{ill|1965|ja|1965年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|ja|2004年}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February qq - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 13 ({{ill|2001|ja|2001年}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 26 ({{ill|1951|ja|1951年}}). Designed by {{ill|Chūta Itō|ja|伊東忠太}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|ja|虎ノ門駅}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|ja|東京メトロ銀座線}} == References == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
