Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigen-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604982}} * February 20 - [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōsai|1=Michiae no matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654797}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|1=Urayasu no mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558194}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinka-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厳島神社}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 13 ([[:en:2001|2001]]) ; Building details : Rebuilt in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 26 ([[:en:1951|1951]]). Designed by [[:en:Itō Chūta|Chūta Itō]], featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden structure|1=wooden structure|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519178}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Toranomon Station|Toranomon Station]] on the [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|version=101929338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Toranomon]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11646485}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646485]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646485]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Toranomon|Toranomon]] 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji (era)]] 3 ([[:en:1660|1660]]) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Toranomon|Toranomon]], [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]]. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toranomon Kotohira Tower|1=Toranomon Kotohira Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690983}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji (era)]] 3 ([[:en:1660|1660]]): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakazu|1=Kyōgoku Takakazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374457}}, the daimyō of the [[:en:Marugame Domain|Marugame Domain]], enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 7 ([[:en:1679|1679]]): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 40 ([[:en:1965|1965]]): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 13 ([[:en:2001|2001]]): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Historical Building|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Government-designated architectural structure of historic value|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525886}}. * Heisei 16 ([[:en:2004|2004]]): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigen-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604982}} * February 20 - [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōsai|1=Michiae no matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654797}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|1=Urayasu no mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558194}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinka-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厳島神社}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 13 ([[:en:2001|2001]]) ; Building details : Rebuilt in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 26 ([[:en:1951|1951]]). Designed by [[:en:Itō Chūta|Chūta Itō]], featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden structure|1=wooden structure|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519178}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Toranomon Station|Toranomon Station]] on the [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|version=101929338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Toranomon]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11646485}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 13 ([[:en:2001|2001]]) ; Building details : Rebuilt in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 26 ([[:en:1951|1951]]). Designed by [[:en:Itō Chūta|Chūta Itō]], featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden structure|1=wooden structure|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519178}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Toranomon Station|Toranomon Station]] on the [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|version=101929338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Toranomon]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11646485}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646485]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646485]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Toranomon|Toranomon]] 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji (era)]] 3 ([[:en:1660|1660]]) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Toranomon|Toranomon]], [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]]. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toranomon Kotohira Tower|1=Toranomon Kotohira Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690983}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji (era)]] 3 ([[:en:1660|1660]]): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakazu|1=Kyōgoku Takakazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374457}}, the daimyō of the [[:en:Marugame Domain|Marugame Domain]], enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 7 ([[:en:1679|1679]]): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 40 ([[:en:1965|1965]]): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 13 ([[:en:2001|2001]]): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Historical Building|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Government-designated architectural structure of historic value|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525886}}. * Heisei 16 ([[:en:2004|2004]]): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigen-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604982}} * February 20 - [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōsai|1=Michiae no matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654797}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|1=Urayasu no mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558194}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinka-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厳島神社}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 13 ([[:en:2001|2001]]) ; Building details : Rebuilt in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 26 ([[:en:1951|1951]]). Designed by [[:en:Itō Chūta|Chūta Itō]], featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden structure|1=wooden structure|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519178}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Toranomon Station|Toranomon Station]] on the [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|version=101929338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Toranomon]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11646485}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigen-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604982}} * February 20 - [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōsai|1=Michiae no matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654797}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|1=Urayasu no mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558194}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinka-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厳島神社}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 13 ([[:en:2001|2001]]) ; Building details : Rebuilt in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 26 ([[:en:1951|1951]]). Designed by [[:en:Itō Chūta|Chūta Itō]], featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden structure|1=wooden structure|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519178}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Toranomon Station|Toranomon Station]] on the [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|version=101929338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11646485}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Toranomon]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646485]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646485]] Wikidata name: Kotohira-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira-gū (金刀比羅宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tranomon Kotohiragu4.JPG|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]], [[:en:Toranomon|Toranomon]] 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji (era)]] 3 ([[:en:1660|1660]]) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Toranomon|Toranomon]], [[:en:港区|Minato, Tokyo]]. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toranomon Kotohira Tower|1=Toranomon Kotohira Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690983}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji (era)]] 3 ([[:en:1660|1660]]): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakazu|1=Kyōgoku Takakazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374457}}, the daimyō of the [[:en:Marugame Domain|Marugame Domain]], enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 7 ([[:en:1679|1679]]): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 40 ([[:en:1965|1965]]): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 13 ([[:en:2001|2001]]): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Historical Building|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Government-designated architectural structure of historic value|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525886}}. * Heisei 16 ([[:en:2004|2004]]): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigen-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604982}} * February 20 - [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōsai|1=Michiae no matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654797}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|1=Urayasu no mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558194}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinka-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厳島神社}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 13 ([[:en:2001|2001]]) ; Building details : Rebuilt in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 26 ([[:en:1951|1951]]). Designed by [[:en:Itō Chūta|Chūta Itō]], featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden structure|1=wooden structure|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519178}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Toranomon Station|Toranomon Station]] on the [[:en:Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|version=101929338|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11646485}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Toranomon]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toranomon Kotohira Tower|1=Toranomon Kotohira Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690983}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakazu|1=Kyōgoku Takakazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374457}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|en|Marugame Domain|WD=Q10863480|lt=Marugame Domain}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}} 7 ({{ill|1679|en|1679|WD=Q7638|lt=1679}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 40 ({{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Historical Building|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Government-designated architectural structure of historic value|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525886}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigen-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604982}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōsai|1=Michiae no matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654797}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|1=Urayasu no mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558194}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinka-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厳島神社}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 26 ({{ill|1951|en|1951|WD=Q18591|lt=1951}}). Designed by {{ill|Itō Chūta|en|Itō Chūta|WD=Q1050457|lt=Chūta Itō}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden structure|1=wooden structure|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519178}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|en|Toranomon Station|WD=Q347972|lt=Toranomon Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|en|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|WD=Q1073046|lt=Tokyo Metro Ginza Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|version=101929338|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11646485}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Toranomon]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toranomon Kotohira Tower|1=Toranomon Kotohira Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690983}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakazu|1=Kyōgoku Takakazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374457}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|en|Marugame Domain|WD=Q10863480|lt=Marugame Domain}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}} 7 ({{ill|1679|en|1679|WD=Q7638|lt=1679}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 40 ({{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Historical Building|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Government-designated architectural structure of historic value|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525886}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toranomon Kotohira Tower|1=Toranomon Kotohira Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690983}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji (era)|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakazu|1=Kyōgoku Takakazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374457}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|en|Marugame Domain|WD=Q10863480|lt=Marugame Domain|1=Marugame Domain}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō|1=Enpō}} 7 ({{ill|1679|en|1679|WD=Q7638|lt=1679|1=1679}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 40 ({{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Historical Building|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Government-designated architectural structure of historic value|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525886}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigensetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604982}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōsai|1=Michiae no matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654797}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|1=Urayasu no mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558194}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinka-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厳島神社}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 26 ({{ill|1951|en|1951|WD=Q18591|lt=1951|1=1951}}). The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toranomon Kotohira Tower|1=Toranomon Kotohira Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690983}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji (era)|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakazu|1=Kyōgoku Takakazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374457}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|en|Marugame Domain|WD=Q10863480|lt=Marugame Domain|1=Marugame Domain}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō|1=Enpō}} 7 ({{ill|1679|en|1679|WD=Q7638|lt=1679|1=1679}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 40 ({{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Historical Building|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Government-designated architectural structure of historic value|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525886}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigensetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604982}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōsai|1=Michiae no matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21654797}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|1=Urayasu no mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558194}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinka-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厳島神社}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|WD=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 26 ({{ill|1951|en|1951|WD=Q18591|lt=1951|1=1951}}). Designed by {{ill|Itō Chūta|en|Itō Chūta|WD=Q1050457|lt=Chūta Itō|1=Itō Chūta}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden structure|1=Q11519178|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519178}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|en|Toranomon Station|WD=Q347972|lt=Toranomon Station|1=Toranomon Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|en|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|WD=Q1073046|lt=Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|1=Ginza Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|version=101929338|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11646485}} [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Toranomon]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|qid=Q1145295|lt=Manji (era)|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|1660|en|1660|qid=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakazu|1=Kyōgoku Takakazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374457}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|en|Marugame Domain|qid=Q10863480|lt=Marugame Domain|1=Marugame Domain}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|qid=Q1145735|lt=Enpō|1=Enpō}} 7 ({{ill|1679|en|1679|qid=Q7638|lt=1679|1=1679}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 40 ({{ill|1965|en|1965|qid=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|qid=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Historical Building|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Government-designated architectural structure of historic value|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11525886}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|en|2004|qid=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigensetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604982}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōsai|1=Michiae no matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21654797}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|1=Urayasu no mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558194}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q72318}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinka-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厳島神社}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|qid=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 26 ({{ill|1951|en|1951|qid=Q18591|lt=1951|1=1951}}). Designed by {{ill|Itō Chūta|en|Itō Chūta|qid=Q1050457|lt=Chūta Itō|1=Itō Chūta}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden structure|1=Q11519178|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11519178}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|en|Toranomon Station|qid=Q347972|lt=Toranomon Station|1=Toranomon Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|en|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|qid=Q1073046|lt=Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|1=Ginza Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|version=101929338|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11646485}} [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Toranomon]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toranomon Kotohira Tower|1=Toranomon Kotohira Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28690983}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|qid=Q1145295|lt=Manji (era)|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|1660|en|1660|qid=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakazu|1=Kyōgoku Takakazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374457}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|en|Marugame Domain|qid=Q10863480|lt=Marugame Domain|1=Marugame Domain}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|qid=Q1145735|lt=Enpō|1=Enpō}} 7 ({{ill|1679|en|1679|qid=Q7638|lt=1679|1=1679}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 40 ({{ill|1965|en|1965|qid=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|qid=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Historical Building|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Government-designated architectural structure of historic value|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11525886}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|en|2004|qid=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigensetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604982}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōsai|1=Michiae no matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21654797}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|1=Urayasu no mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558194}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q72318}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinka-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厳島神社}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|qid=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 26 ({{ill|1951|en|1951|qid=Q18591|lt=1951|1=1951}}). The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|en|Toranomon Station|qid=Q347972|lt=Toranomon Station|1=Toranomon Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|en|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|qid=Q1073046|lt=Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|1=Ginza Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|version=101929338|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)]] {{Commons category|Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11646485}} [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Toranomon]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|10.6|N|139|44|52.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|港区|en|港区|qid=Q14219833|lt=Minato, Tokyo|1=港区}}, {{ill|Toranomon|en|Toranomon|qid=Q7825412|lt=Toranomon|1=Toranomon}} 1-2-7 | Latitude = 35 | Lat_min = 40 | Lat_sec = 10.6 | Longitude = 139 | Lon_min = 44 | Lon_sec = 52.8 | Map = Tokyo city | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} | SacredObject = | Classification = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | Established = {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|qid=Q1145295|lt=Manji (era)|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|1660|en|1660|qid=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Toranomon Kotohira-gū | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira-gū''' (金刀比羅宮) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in {{ill|Toranomon|en|Toranomon|qid=Q7825412|lt=Toranomon|1=Toranomon}}, {{ill|港区|en|港区|qid=Q14219833|lt=Minato, Tokyo|1=港区}}. The shrine grounds also host the high-rise office building {{ill|Toranomon Kotohira Tower|ja|虎ノ門琴平タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toranomon Kotohira Tower|1=Toranomon Kotohira Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28690983}}, which integrates facilities such as the shrine office and the Kagura hall. The shrine is also referred to as '''Toranomon Kotohira-gū''' (虎ノ門金刀比羅宮), reflecting its location. == History == * {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|qid=Q1145295|lt=Manji (era)|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|1660|en|1660|qid=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}}): {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakazu|1=Kyōgoku Takakazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374457}}, the daimyō of the {{ill|Marugame Domain|en|Marugame Domain|qid=Q10863480|lt=Marugame Domain|1=Marugame Domain}}, enshrined {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} at the clan's Edo residence in Shiba-Mita. * {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|qid=Q1145735|lt=Enpō|1=Enpō}} 7 ({{ill|1679|en|1679|qid=Q7638|lt=1679|1=1679}}): Relocated to the current location in Toranomon following the relocation of the Marugame Domain Edo residence. * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 40 ({{ill|1965|en|1965|qid=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan building constructed. * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|qid=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}): Designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Historical Building|ja|東京都選定歴史的建造物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Historical Building|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Government-designated architectural structure of historic value|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11525886}}. * Heisei 16 ({{ill|2004|en|2004|qid=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}}): Toranomon Konpira Kaikan was rebuilt as Toranomon Kotohira Tower. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} == festivals == ; Monthly rituals {{Table 2 columns| * 1st - Tsuitachi-sai (New Month Festival) * 10th - Tōka-sai (Monthly Festival) | * 9th - Kokonoka-sai * 20th - Hatsuka-sai }} ; Other festivals * January 1 - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * January 9 - Hatsukonpira Eve Festival * January 10 - Hatsukonpira Festival: Edo folk dances and Kagura performances, along with a Seven Gods of Fortune procession and vendor stalls. * February 3 - Setsubun Tsuina Festival and bean-throwing ceremony at the Kagura hall. * February 11 - {{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigensetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604982}} * February 20 - {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) and celebration for {{ill|Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja|喜代住稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyozumi Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (sub-shrine). * June 30 - {{ill|Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja|夏越大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Purification rites and {{ill|Dōkyōsai|ja|道饗祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōsai|1=Michiae no matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21654797}}. * October 9 - Grand Festival Eve * October 10 - Grand Festival: Dedication of the {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|1=Urayasu no mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558194}} dance, Okame and Hyottoko processions, and vendor stalls. * November 23 - {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * December 23 - {{ill|Tenchōsai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q72318}} * December 30 - Year-End Ōharae, {{ill|Chinka-sai|ja|鎮火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinka-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厳島神社}}, and New Year's Eve Ceremony. == Cultural Properties == ; Tokyo Selected Historical Building : Designated in {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 13 ({{ill|2001|en|2001|qid=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}) ; Building details : Rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 26 ({{ill|1951|en|1951|qid=Q18591|lt=1951|1=1951}}). Designed by {{ill|Itō Chūta|en|Itō Chūta|qid=Q1050457|lt=Chūta Itō|1=Itō Chūta}}, featuring a {{ill|wooden structure|ja|木構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden structure|1=Q11519178|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11519178}}. The worship and offering halls follow the gongen-zukuri architectural style.[https://www.toshiseibi.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/kenchiku/keikan/list_rekisi.htm Tokyo Metropolitan Bureau of Urban Development - List of Selected Historical Buildings] == Gallery == File:Toranomon Kotohiragu1.JPG|View of the First Torii from the main road (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu2.JPG|First Torii leading to the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu3.JPG|Chōzuya (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu5.JPG|Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu6.JPG|South-East view of the Worship Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu7.JPG|Kagura Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) File:Toranomon Kotohiragu8.JPG|North side passage near the Main Hall (Photo taken May 14, 2010) == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Toranomon Station|en|Toranomon Station|qid=Q347972|lt=Toranomon Station|1=Toranomon Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|en|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|qid=Q1073046|lt=Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|1=Ginza Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.kotohira.or.jp/ Official website of Toranomon Kotohira-gū] * [http://www.kotohiratower.com/ Toranomon Kotohira Tower (Mitsui Fudosan)] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|version=101929338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11646485|co|commons:Category:Kotohira-gu (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)}} [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Marugame Domain]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Toranomon]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
